card.intuition: React's strategy of maintaining a lightweight copy of the UI in memory (Virtual DOM) and comparing it with the previous version to find the minimum number of changes needed to update the real DOM.
card.analogy: Building a Lego castle based on a photo: if you want to change the color of the drawbridge, you don't tear down the whole castle. You compare the new photo (New VDOM) with the old one (Old VDOM), see that ONLY the drawbridge changed, and swap only those specific blocks.
when_to_use: Understanding how React optimizes UI updates
Debugging why a component is re-rendering unexpectedly
Optimizing performance in large, complex list views
gotchas: The Virtual DOM is NOT faster than the real DOM; it's an abstraction that finds the most efficient way to UPDATE the real DOM
Using 'index' as a key in lists can confuse the reconciliation algorithm if the list order changes, leading to UI bugs

exercise.prompt: Identify the standard React property used to help the reconciliation algorithm identify which items in a list have changed, been added, or been removed.
hints: It's a 'key' part of list rendering
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "key"
assert check("key")
print("PASS")
